oaxacan-tamales-recipe-comedera.name: Oaxacan Tamales Recipe

ingredients.item: 500 gr de carne de cerdo o pollo deshebrado

instructions: Beat the butter till it's fluffy.
Once ready, it incorporates the dough of corn little by little, alternating with chicken stock, baking powder and salt. The mass should be soft and light.
Cooking pork or chicken till tender, shedding and booking.
Warm up the oaxaquan mole and add the broken meat and mix well till well integrated.
Cut banana leaves into rectangles about 30 cm by 20 cm.
Put a portion of mass at the centre of each leaf and light up. Add a tablespoon of mole with meat at the centre of the dough.
Fold the sides of the banana leaf to the centre and then turn the ends down, and form a well wrapped package.
Coloca los tamales en una vaporera, con la parte doblada hacia abajo.
Fitted steamed for about 1 hour and 30 minutes, or until the dough is firm and easily clear from the banana leaf.

obatzda-the-bavarian-cream-cheese-spread.name: Obatzda, The Bavarian Cream Cheese Spread
obatzda-the-bavarian-cream-cheese-spread.original_name: Obatzda, la crema de queso bávara para untar

ingredients.item: 200 g de queso camembert o queso brie
2 cucharadas de mantequilla sin sal, a temperatura ambiente

instructions: At a large bowl, place Camembert or Brie and cream cheese. Be sure they're at room temperature to facilitate their blend.
with a fork, pits cheeses to get a smooth and homogenous blend.
Add minced onion and butter to room temperature. Keep mixing till all ingredients are well combined.
Incorporates sweet pimenton and ground cumin into the mixture. Remix to distribute flavors evenly.
Test the mixture and bowl with salt and pepper to taste. Adjust the condiments to your personal preferences.
Transfiere el Obatzda a un recipiente adecuado para servir y decora con cebollino picado si lo deseas.
Cover the container with film paper and refrigerate for at least 1 hour so that flavors blend and cool properly.
obatzda-the-bavarian-cream-cheese-spread.category: Recetas de Aperitivos, Tapas y Pinchos

old-hamburger-bun-croutons.name: Old Hamburger Bun Croutons

ingredients.item: Coarsely ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Drizzle olive oil over hamburger bun cubs in a large bowl; toss to coat. Spread cubes onto a baking sheet. Sprinkle garlic powder, oregano, thyme, basil, salt, and black pepper over the cubes.
Bake in the preheated oven until brown and crispy, 8 to 10 minutes. Cool completely before storing in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks.

omelette-cups.name: Omelette Cups
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper.
Push 3 tater tots in each cup of a non-stick muffin tin.
Bake at 218°C (220°C) for 10 minutes. Remove from oven.
Place your favorite omelette fillings in each muffin tin on top of the tater tots.
Pour egg mixture into each muffin tin.
Bake at 177°C (177°C) for 20 minutes, or until fully cooked!
Enjoy!

one-pan-pesto-chicken-veggies.name: One-pan Pesto Chicken & Veggies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200°C). Line a baking sheet with foil.
Lay out the 3 chicken breasts in the middle of the baking tray. Spread the zucchini evenly on one end and the cherry tomatoes on the other.
Sprinkle the garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper evenly over the chicken and vegetables. Toss the vegetables to coat evenly.
Pour the jar of pesto evenly over the chicken breasts, then top with the mozzarella cheese.
Bake for 25–30 minutes, until the internal temperature of the chicken reaches 74°C (75°C) and the vegetables are tender.
Let the chicken rest for 10 minutes before serving over the vegetables with another sprinkle of mozzarella cheese.
Enjoy!

one-pan-pork-chops-with-creamy-mushroom-.name: One-pan Pork Chops With Creamy Mushroom Sauce

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Heat butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown pork chops in the hot butter, sprinkling both sides with salt and pepper, about 5 minutes per side. Remove meat to a plate and set aside.

Cook onion and garlic in the pan drippings over medium-high heat until softened, about 3 minutes. Stir in wine, reduce heat, and let simmer until alcohol smell is completely cooked off, 3 to 4 minutes. Stir in soup and milk, blending well. Layer mushrooms on top of soup mixture, add chops on top of mushrooms, and pour meat drippings from the plate over the chops. Sprinkle with thyme and sage.
Cover tightly and let simmer until chops are tender and no longer pink in the centers, about 30 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read 63°C (63 degrees C).

orzo-and-broccoli-salad-no-mayo.name: Orzo And Broccoli Salad (no Mayo)

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add orzo and cook for 8 minutes. Add broccoli florets to the pot and cook until tender, 2 more minutes. Drain and transfer to a large bowl.

Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-low heat. Add pine nuts and cook until browned, about 3 minutes. Add red pepper flakes; cook and stir until aromatic, about 30 seconds. Remove from the heat and pour over the orzo.
Toss orzo until coated with oil mixture. Add olives, Parmesan cheese, basil, and feta cheese. Toss again and season with salt and pepper.

orzo-and-wild-rice-salad.name: Orzo And Wild Rice Salad
instructions: Bring 2 cups water and wild rice to a boil in a saucepan; reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer until rice is tender but not mushy, 20 to 45 minutes, depending on rice variety and package directions.
Drain excess liquid, fluff rice with a fork, and continue to cook, uncovered, 5 minutes more. Spread in a shallow dish, cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until cold, about 20 minutes.

Meanwhile, bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook orzo in the boiling water, stirring occasionally until tender yet firm to the bite, 7 to 8 minutes. Drain orzo, rinse with cold water, then refrigerate to chill.
Combine chilled rice and orzo in a large bowl; stir in onion, currants, bell peppers, and corn. Season with 2 tablespoons basil, salt, and black pepper.

Whisk vinegar, honey, remaining ½ tablespoon basil, mustard, and garlic together in a separate bowl; slowly whisk in canola and olive oils until emulsified. Stir vinaigrette into rice-orzo mixture; season with salt and black pepper, if desired. Refrigerate before serving for 2 hours.
